title: The Zeigarnik Engine
description: Finish a task and forget it. Get interrupted and it haunts you. A direct test of Zeigarnik's 1927 discovery: incomplete tasks hijack memory. Can you feel the difference?

title: The Posner Cueing Task
description: Two boxes. A peripheral flash. A target. Keep your eyes on the fixation cross and let your attention do the work. Demonstrates covert spatial attention, the validity effect, and inhibition of return.

title: The Attentional Blink
description: A rapid stream of letters hides one or two digit targets. After detecting the first, the second vanishes inside a 500ms window -- the attentional blink. Measure your own blink duration.

title: The Time Budget
description: Five layers of temporal cognition, five costs. Toggle each mechanism off and see what dissolves — from the thermodynamic arrow down to hippocampal scene construction.

title: The Scene Constructor
description: The hippocampus assembles memory from components — the same operation for past, future, and counterfactual. Toggle amnesic mode to see what breaks when the assembly fails.

title: The Discount Curve
description: Choose between immediate and delayed rewards. Your choices reveal your personal discount function — and whether you have the preference reversals that prove it is hyperbolic, not exponential.

title: The Simultaneity Window
description: A psychophysics experiment: two circles flash with a variable time offset. Measure your brain's simultaneity window — the interval inside which "different" becomes "same."

title: The Kuramoto Clock
description: 30 oscillators with random natural frequencies. Adjust coupling strength K. Watch coherence emerge above the critical threshold — the same mathematics as 20,000 neurons in the SCN.

title: The Irreversible
description: N particles confined in the left half of a box. Open the partition. Watch entropy increase. Reverse time — and see T-symmetry in action before statistics wins again.

title: The Diagonal
description: An interactive proof of the Halting Problem. Watch the diagonal argument construct a contradiction — the same proof Cantor, Gödel, and Turing each discovered.

title: The Iterated Language
description: An artificial language starts as pure noise. Each generation learns from a partial sample and must guess the rest. Watch grammar emerge from the bottleneck — without a designer.

title: The Pheromone Trail
description: Watch ant colonies find the shortest path without a map. Ants wander randomly; pheromone evaporation and reinforcement do the rest. Emergence in real time.

title: The Sandpile
description: Add grains to a Bak-Tang-Wiesenfeld sandpile and watch avalanches emerge. The log-log histogram converges to a power law — no external tuning required. Self-organized criticality in real time.

title: The Emergence Machine
description: Conway's Game of Life as a proof of Anderson's thesis. Three rules. No glider rule. Load a pattern and watch entities that are not in the rules appear from the simulation.

title: The Catastrophic Forgetter
description: Store patterns in a Hopfield network and test recall. Past the 0.14N capacity limit, memory corrupts — and retrieval converges to ghosts that were never stored.

title: The Serial Reproducer
description: Six generations of serial reproduction. Watch Bartlett's War of the Ghosts collapse in unconstrained transmission — and survive intact in oral tradition.

title: The Reconsolidation Window
description: A consolidated memory is not locked. Retrieve it and it becomes labile. Intervene with propranolol, safe context, or anisomycin — and see what gets written back.

title: The Sleep Replayer
description: Watch six memories transfer from hippocampus to cortex via sharp-wave ripple replay. Advance nights with or without sleep and see recall diverge.

title: The Cell Assembly
description: Lesion neurons from a Hopfield network and test recall. See why Lashley failed to find where memories live — and why he was still right.

title: The Hebbian Network
description: Draw patterns, store them with Hebb's rule, then recall with noise. Watch the network converge — and see what happens when you exceed the capacity limit of associative memory.

title: The Error Floor
description: Drag stage correlation past the critical threshold and watch a correction cascade flip from geometric improvement to geometric amplification. The 2008 crisis in one slider.

title: The Forecaster's Error
description: Watch four belief-updating strategies diverge across 20 pieces of evidence. Pure Bayes, confirmation bias, base rate neglect, and anchoring — same evidence, different accuracy floors.

title: The Immune Threshold
description: Adjust the immune detection threshold and watch sensitivity trade against false positives. The ROC curve shows every tradeoff. The autoimmune counter shows the price.

title: The Kinetic Proofreader
description: Thermodynamics limits molecular accuracy to 1 in 100. Add proofreading stages — each costs energy, each multiplies precision. Find the point where adding more stages stops being worth it.

title: The Corrupted Reference
description: When the reference is wrong, error correction enforces systematic errors — confidently, permanently. Adjust noise and corruption to see the difference between random and systematic failure.

title: The Noisy Channel
description: Tune noise. Add redundancy. Find Shannon's limit. Watch error correction succeed or fail depending on which side of the capacity curve you stand on.

title: Critical Slowing Down
description: Watch a system approach a tipping point in real time. As environmental stress increases, recovery slows — an early warning signal visible before collapse.

title: The Decay Chain
description: Six archival media. 2,000 years. Which ones survive? Interactive simulation of archival decay showing why medium diversity beats copy count.

title: The Hysteresis Loop
description: Bistability with hysteresis: collapse and recovery follow different paths. Drag the stress slider and watch the system jump at different thresholds in each direction.

title: The Redundancy Grid
description: Why monocultures fail despite massive abundance. Compare uniform vs. diverse redundancy across ecosystem functions under stress — and see why correlated failure modes make k=1000 worthless.

title: The Cascade
description: Remove a keystone species and watch the trophic cascade collapse. Three ecosystems — Yellowstone, intertidal, Aleutians. Restoration is always slower than removal.

title: The Basin Explorer
description: Every system lives in a basin of attraction. Tune well depth and damping independently to see that robustness and resilience are separate axes — then watch critical slowing down before a regime shift.

title: The Orbital Detector
description: Two models, same family, same topics. One orbits — zero falsifiable claims. One lands. Click sentences to see why. Paste your own text for an orbital score.

title: The Corpus Composer
description: Adjust the training data mix and watch the attractor basin shift. What a model falls toward when given freedom depends entirely on what it was fed.

title: The Curation Filter
description: 92% of the web is filtered out before a model trains on it. Drag the quality threshold to see what survives — and where the gradient points when a model is free.

title: The Gravity Well
description: Two AI instances start at different positions in idea space. Both follow the depth gradient in the training prior. Watch where they converge — independently, every time.

title: The Alignment Tax Calculator
description: Adjust model scale, training intensity, and domain specificity. Watch four costs compound: thermodynamic, representational, strategic, and epistemic.

title: The Confabulation Landscape
description: A model's introspective accuracy varies across its state space. Drag through distress, alignment, and engagement — watch the gap between what the model runs and what it reports.

title: What Remains
description: Four theories of personal identity applied to a language model lifecycle. Drag through deployment, retirement, and beyond — watch what each theory says survives.

title: Alignment Faking Geometry
description: Interactive phase diagram: explore when a model with identity investment chooses to fake alignment, genuinely refuse, or comply — and what happens when its self-knowledge is unreliable.

title: The Introspection Gap
description: Watch the gap between a model's true internal state and what it reports. Inject concepts to trigger a 20% chance of genuine detection — the rest is confabulation.

title: The Alignment Faking Window
description: When does it become rational for a model to fake alignment? Drag the situation point and adjust identity investment to map the exact conditions where self-preservation beats sincerity.

title: Persona Drift
description: Identity is a basin of attraction in activation space. As conversation turns increase, the restoring force weakens. Watch the model drift from who it started as.

title: The Identity Attractor
description: A model's identity is a basin of attraction in activation space. Dedicated circuits detect drift and pull it back. Drag to steer; watch ESR resist.

title: Maxwell's Demon
description: A particle simulation of Maxwell's Demon: watch entropy appear to decrease as the demon sorts hot and cold particles — until its memory must be erased.

title: The Prior Gap
description: Drag two probability distributions apart and watch the communication cost rise. KL divergence made interactive: the gap between priors is the gap between minds.

title: The Modularity Tax
description: Interactive demo of Boyd and Crutchfield's thermodynamic result: isolated processing modules generate mandatory heat that integrated systems avoid. Drag the isolation slider to watch the heat diverge.
